seo.title: Building Research Agents with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Instructor simplifies building Research Agents by patching LLM clients to return validated Pydantic models, ensuring structured outputs from web search synthesis tasks. Guardrails are essential for multi-step agents that perform source verification, claim accuracy checks, recency validation, and bias detection. Vex integration prevents common Instructor failures like schema violations and hallucinated enum values, maintaining agent reliability.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents typically follow a multi-step pipeline: 1) Query decomposition into sub-questions, 2) Web search execution with source retrieval, 3) Content extraction and structured parsing using Instructor's Pydantic models, 4) Claim verification against sources, and 5) Bias/recency analysis before final synthesis. Instructor wraps the LLM client to enforce schemas for extracted facts, sources, and summaries, preventing partial or malformed outputs.

The agent orchestrates these steps iteratively, using structured outputs to maintain state and validate intermediate results. Common patterns include SourceFact models with URL, excerpt, and recency fields, plus ClaimVerification models scoring accuracy and bias. This ensures downstream synthesis builds on verified data rather than hallucinated content.

Vex enhances this by verifying critical outputs like source authenticity and claim validity, catching issues Instructor's schema validation misses.

risks.name: Structured output validation retries exhausted
risks.description: Instructor retries parsing when LLM outputs don't match Pydantic schema, but exhausts after max attempts, leaving agent without structured facts
risks.mitigation: Wrap with Vex.verify() to validate content before schema parsing and provide fallback prompts

risks.name: Partial extraction missing required fields
risks.description: LLM extracts some source facts but omits required fields like 'recency' or 'confidence_score', breaking downstream verification
risks.mitigation: Vex source verification ensures all critical fields are populated with valid data before Instructor processing

risks.name: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
risks.description: LLM generates invalid 'bias_type' values like 'political_left' when schema expects 'neutral|positive|negative', causing parsing failures
risks.mitigation: Vex claim accuracy checks validate enum values against real sources before Instructor validation

risks.name: Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
risks.description: Source citation objects have varying nesting levels (some have 'page_num', others don't), breaking uniform fact verification
risks.mitigation: Instructor's partial=True mode + Vex recency checks normalize nested structures across sources

implementation.title: Query Decomposition with Instructor
implementation.description: Split complex research queries into verifiable sub-questions using Pydantic models
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from openai import OpenAI

class SubQuery(BaseModel):
    question: str
    search_query: str = Field(..., description="Optimized search terms")
    requires_verification: bool

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
sub_queries = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=[SubQuery],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Break down: What are the latest AI safety benchmarks?"}]
)

for q in sub_queries:
    print(f"Search: {q.search_query} (verify: {q.requires_verification})")

implementation.title: Source Fact Extraction
implementation.description: Extract structured facts from search results with source attribution
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, HttpUrl
from typing import List, Optional
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI

class SourceFact(BaseModel):
    claim: str
    source_url: HttpUrl
    excerpt: str
    recency_days: int = Field(..., ge=0)
    confidence: float = Field(..., ge=0, le=1)

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
facts = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=List[SourceFact],
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Extract facts from: [search results]"},
        {"role": "system", "content": "Always include recency_days from publication date"}
    ]
)

implementation.title: TypeScript Source Verification
implementation.description: Validate extracted sources using Instructor's Zod integration

implementation.title: Claim Synthesis with Bias Check
implementation.description: Synthesize verified claims with bias detection
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel
from enum import Enum
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI

class BiasType(str, Enum):
    NEUTRAL = "neutral"
    POSITIVE = "positive"
    NEGATIVE = "negative"

class ResearchSummary(BaseModel):
    summary: str
    key_claims: list[str]
    bias_detected: BiasType
    sources_cited: int

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
summary = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=ResearchSummary,
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Synthesize these verified facts:"},
        {"role": "system", "content": "Use only provided sources, detect bias"}
    ]
)
print(f"Summary: {summary.summary} (Bias: {summary.bias_detected})")

vexIntegration.title: Vex Source Verification Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Verify source authenticity before Instructor parsing
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel
from openai import OpenAI

@vex.verify(criteria=["source_verification", "recency_checks"])
def extract_trusted_facts(content: str, sources: list) -> list[dict]:
    client = from_openai(OpenAI())
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        response_model=list,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Extract from verified sources: {content}"}]
    )

# Usage
facts = extract_trusted_facts(search_results, source_urls)
# Vex auto-retries if sources fail verification
